message:
fix(replay_capture): poison-tolerant pane_sequences lock (ft-xvrlp)

`fn next_pane_seq` at replay_capture.rs:209 previously called
`.lock().unwrap()` on the per-pane sequence Mutex. Every
capture_egress / capture_ingress / capture_lifecycle / capture_control
/ capture_decision routes through this method, so a single panic in
another thread holding the lock would abort the recorder for the rest
of the process — turning one buggy caller into a recorder-wide outage.

## Fix

Replace `.lock().unwrap()` with
`.lock().unwrap_or_else(std::sync::PoisonError::into_inner)`. This is
the well-established Rust idiom for "recover the inner data from a
poisoned lock and continue". Safe in this code path because:

- The protected `HashMap<u64, AtomicU64>` is structurally consistent
  even if a panic interrupts another caller. `entry().or_insert_with`
  is atomic w.r.t. the mutex (no torn allocation), and `AtomicU64` is
  itself internally atomic so any in-flight `fetch_add` is either
  fully observed or not started.
- The only invariant the lock enforces is single-writer access to
  the map's internal hashing structure, which `PoisonError::into_inner`
  restores intact.

Added doc comment on `next_pane_seq` explaining the rationale so the
next reader doesn't roll back to `.unwrap()`.

## Regression test

`next_pane_seq_recovers_from_poisoned_mutex` (in mod tests at the
bottom of the file):
- Wraps a CaptureAdapter in Arc, captures a baseline egress event.
- Spawns a thread that takes the pane_sequences lock and panics —
  poisoning it.
- Asserts `pane_sequences.is_poisoned() == true`.
- Calls capture_egress on the SAME pane (exercises the
  `entry().or_insert_with` path through the recovered guard).
- Calls capture_egress on a FRESH pane id (forces a new HashMap
  insert through the recovered guard, proving the map itself is
  structurally usable post-poison).
- Asserts the sink received all three events.

Pre-fix this test would abort the test thread on the first
post-poison capture call.

Out of scope: the four other `.lock().unwrap()` sites in this file
(lines 73, 88, 98, 104) are on `CollectingCaptureSink` — a test/
collect-only sink, not the production hot path. The bead's scope is
the production line 209 only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
